description: The screening engine's rendered disposition for a customer, as produced by the external system of record (OpenSanctions yente /match scores candidates against OFAC SDN, EU and UN sanctions, and PEP datasets, at a specific data/list version). Notarize-only: AGLedger notarizes what the screening engine rendered; the screening engine renders the match. This is the screening-engine seam, capturing which subject was screened, against which list version, with which scorer, and the top match score the engine returned. A match:false / low-score screen can terminate clean (auto-clear at the gate); a match:true / high-score screen is the contested input that the engine gate routes to a human analyst. The list version is load-bearing for audit: regulators want to know which snapshot a hit was rendered against.

onboardingCaseRef.description: recordId of the keystone-onboarding-case-v1 this screen belongs to.

screeningProvider.description: The external screening system of record, e.g. 'OpenSanctions yente'.

datasetVersion.description: The list/dataset snapshot the disposition was rendered against (yente returns the dataset version) — the audit anchor for 'which snapshot'.

scope.description: The screening scope/dataset the engine rendered against, captured verbatim (e.g. yente 'default' = sanctions+PEPs+risk, 'sanctions', 'peps', or a deployment's custom collection name). Free-form on purpose: a closed enum would reject a faithful disposition rendered against a dataset this recipe did not anticipate.

scorer.description: The scoring algorithm/model the engine applied, captured verbatim (yente scorers include logic-v2 (default-equivalent multi-feature), logic-v1, name-based, name-qualified, and regression-v1). Free-form so a deployment on a different screening vendor is not forced into one engine's names.

topScore.description: Highest candidate match score the engine returned (0.0-1.0).

matchCount.description: Number of candidates the engine returned above its own retrieval floor.

match.description: The engine's boolean disposition on the top candidate (yente results[].match).

topMatchId.description: OpenSanctions entity id of the top candidate (cross-reference into the screening system of record).

topMatchCaption.description: Display caption of the top candidate, e.g. 'Alexander Vyacheslavovich ZAKHAROV'.

topMatchDatasets.description: Which list(s) the top candidate is on (e.g. ['us_ofac_sdn','eu_fsf']).

explanationsDigest.description: Hex digest of the yente per-feature explanations payload — binds the 'why' of the match without putting the full breakdown on-chain.
